Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.debug

Class FlixelDebugManager

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.debug.FlixelDebugManager
public class FlixelDebugManager extends Object
The single entry point for everything related to the FlixelGDX debugger.

An instance of this class is automatically created when Flixel.initialize(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelGame) runs and is exposed as the static field Flixel.debug, mirroring how Flixel.sound, Flixel.assets, and friends work. From your game code you can do things like: 


 Flixel.debug.toggleVisible();
 Flixel.debug.setDrawDebug(true);
 Flixel.debug.registerCommand("hello", args -> Flixel.info("Hi!"));
 Flixel.debug.executeCommand("hello");

The manager is intentionally lightweight: it forwards visibility/hitbox toggles to the active FlixelDebugOverlay, owns the registry of console commands, and tracks the "currently inspected sprite" for the texture inspector window. The overlay itself (and the platform-specific UI) reads from the manager rather than the other way around so the manager can stay platform-agnostic and reflection-free.

Custom commands

Use registerCommand(String, Consumer) with a handler that receives FlixelDebugCommandArgs. That keeps parsing explicit and avoids reflection, which is important on platforms where reflection is restricted (TeaVM, R8/ProGuard-shrunk Android builds).

Thread safety

All public methods are intended to be called from the libGDX main thread. Reading the registered commands map outside the main thread is unsupported.

    static final int
    MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES
    Maximum entries kept in the input history (oldest are dropped first).

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    FlixelDebugManager()
    Internal: registers the small set of always-available commands (help, clear, etc.).

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    executeCommand(@NotNull String commandLine)
    Executes a raw command line.
    @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Array<String>
    getCommandHistory()
    Returns the in-memory command history (oldest first).
    @Nullable FlixelObject
    getDraggedSprite()
    Returns the sprite that is currently being dragged via the LMB picker, or null.
    @Nullable FlixelObject
    getInspectedSprite()
    Returns the sprite currently inspected, or null if no sprite is selected (or the previously selected sprite has been destroyed).
    @Nullable FlixelDebugOverlay
    getOverlay()
    Returns the currently active FlixelDebugOverlay, or null if the game is not running in debug mode (or the overlay has not been created yet).
    @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Array<String>
    getRegisteredCommandNames()
    Returns the Array of registered command names.
    boolean

    Returns true if a command with the given name is registered.

    Returns true when bounding-box drawing (hitboxes) is on.

      public static final int MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES
      Maximum entries kept in the input history (oldest are dropped first).

      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • FlixelDebugManager

      public FlixelDebugManager()
      Internal: registers the small set of always-available commands (help, clear, etc.).

  • Method Details

    • getOverlay

      @Nullable public @Nullable FlixelDebugOverlay getOverlay()
      Returns the currently active FlixelDebugOverlay, or null if the game is not running in debug mode (or the overlay has not been created yet).
      Returns:
      The active overlay, or null.

    • isVisible

      public boolean isVisible()
      Returns true when the overlay is on screen.

    • setVisible

      public void setVisible(boolean visible)
      Shows or hides the overlay.
      Parameters:
      visible - true to show, false to hide.

    • toggleVisible

      public void toggleVisible()
      Toggles the overlay visibility.

    • isDrawDebug

      public boolean isDrawDebug()
      Returns true when bounding-box drawing (hitboxes) is on.

    • setDrawDebug

      public void setDrawDebug(boolean drawDebug)
      Sets whether bounding boxes (hitboxes) should be drawn.
      Parameters:
      drawDebug - true to draw, false to hide.

    • toggleDrawDebug

      public void toggleDrawDebug()
      Toggles bounding-box drawing.

    • setInspectedSprite

      public void setInspectedSprite(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelObject obj)
      Sets the sprite currently inspected by the texture inspector window. Pass null to clear the selection.
      Parameters:
      obj - The sprite to inspect, or null to clear.

    • getInspectedSprite

      @Nullable public @Nullable FlixelObject getInspectedSprite()
      Returns the sprite currently inspected, or null if no sprite is selected (or the previously selected sprite has been destroyed). The returned sprite may be of any subclass of FlixelObject.
      Returns:
      The inspected sprite, or null.

    • getDraggedSprite

      @Nullable public @Nullable FlixelObject getDraggedSprite()
      Returns the sprite that is currently being dragged via the LMB picker, or null.

    • registerCommand

      public void registerCommand(@NotNull @NotNull String name, @NotNull @NotNull Consumer<FlixelDebugCommandArgs> handler)
      Registers a console command. The handler receives a FlixelDebugCommandArgs object that wraps the positional tokens the user typed after the command name.

      Example: 

      
 Flixel.debug.registerCommand("hello", args -> {
   String name = args.getString(0, "World"); // 0 = The first argument after the command name.
   Flixel.info("Hello, " + name + "!");
 });
      Parameters:
      name - The command name (the first token typed in the console).
      handler - The handler invoked when the command runs.

    • unregisterCommand

      public void unregisterCommand(@NotNull @NotNull String name)
      Removes a previously registered command.
      Parameters:
      name - The command name to remove.

    • executeCommand

      public boolean executeCommand(@NotNull @NotNull String commandLine)
      Executes a raw command line. The first whitespace-separated token is the command name and any remaining tokens become positional arguments. Logs an error to Flixel.log if the command does not exist.
      Parameters:
      commandLine - The raw input line (for example "spawn enemy.png 1.5").
      Returns:
      true if a command matched and was executed; false otherwise.

    • getCommandHistory

      @NotNull public @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Array<String> getCommandHistory()
      Returns the in-memory command history (oldest first). The returned array is the live backing store and must not be modified. Use Array.size and indexed access to read it.
      Returns:
      The command history (live, do not modify).

    • getRegisteredCommandNames

      @NotNull public @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Array<String> getRegisteredCommandNames()
      Returns the Array of registered command names. The returned array is freshly allocated.

    • hasCommand

      public boolean hasCommand(@NotNull @NotNull String name)
      Returns true if a command with the given name is registered.
